title: OpenClaw MCP Streamable HTTP transport forwarded operator-configured custom headers across cross-origin redirects
description: When constructing the MCP Streamable HTTP transport in resolveMcpTransport(), OpenClaw passes operator-configured custom headers via requestInit.headers but supplies no custom fetch implementation, so the default StreamableHTTPClientTransport fetch transparently follows HTTP redirects and re-sends those headers to the redirect target. If a configured MCP endpoint (transportType 'streamable-http' with sensitive headers under mcp.servers.*.headers) is malicious, compromised, or otherwise issues a cross-origin redirect, sensitive custom headers such as API keys or tenant-routing headers are leaked to a different origin. The SSE transport branch by contrast already wires a controlled fetch, but the streamable-http branch does not.
